title: Dynamic Observation of a Client with Chronologically Problematic Cuticles: Management Protocol, Procedure Interval, Effectiveness Criteria, and the Role of Home Care in Restoring the Skin's Barrier Properties
author: Svitlana, Motyl
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The present study is oriented toward a comprehensive examination of the problem of dynamic follow-up in clients who exhibit chronic destructive alterations of the cuticle and proximal/lateral nail folds. The analysis addresses the current state of the global professional nail-care market, identifies leading etiopathogenetic factors that underlie the development of skin barrier dysfunction in the periungual zone, and substantiates the rationale for shifting emphasis away from aggressive aesthetic interventions toward therapeutically calibrated restoration protocols. The central objective is the development and subsequent validation of a scientific–practical management algorithm for clients with long-standing, persistently “problematic” cuticles, along with a justification of optimal inter-procedure intervals using transepidermal water loss (TEWL) as an objective marker of epidermal barrier status. Methodologically, the study is grounded in a systematized review of current dermatological sources, interpretation of clinically meaningful metrics, and the application of specialized scales intended for quality-of-life assessment, specifically NAIL-Q (QPortfolio, n.d.). Within the analytical framework, findings were obtained indicating a biochemically mediated effectiveness of urea, ceramides, and natural oils with respect to restoring the lipid matrix of the stratum corneum and supporting regenerative processes. These positions support the hypothesis that achieving full functional rehabilitation of the nail apparatus requires prolonged monitoring and targeted correction of the lipid profile, while the expected time horizon for stable recovery is approximately 3–6 months. The presented materials have applied relevance for professionals working in manicure services, podiatry, and dermatology who rely on scientifically grounded practical solutions.
